documents.title: GOVERNANCE ARCHITECTURE MAP
documents.description: Conceptual architecture map for authority, gates, pipeline state, and artifact traceability.
documents.claim_supported: AETHERUS has a coherent governance architecture for controlled LLM and agent workflow concepts.
documents.boundary: Not a deployed product map, implementation audit, or certification package.
documents.body:
# System Overview

Object status: **Conceptual architecture**.

AETHERUS MONOLITH frames controlled LLM and agent workflow concepts through five concerns:

- **Authority**: human permission and override boundaries.
- **Governance Gates**: validation before downstream release.
- **Pipeline State**: visible PASS, FAIL, FREEZE, REPAIR, ESCALATE, and AUDIT movement.
- **Artifacts**: traceable schemas, templates, and deltas.
- **Control Surface**: a planned interface direction for human review.

## Scope

This is research and prototype-facing material. It is not presented as an already deployed enterprise SaaS platform.

documents.title: AUTHORITY CONTROL MODEL
documents.description: Methodology for keeping agent action tied to human authority, documented overrides, and review boundaries.
documents.claim_supported: Agent workflow concepts should remain subordinate to explicit human authority and documented override rules.
documents.boundary: Not proof of an active identity system, access-control backend, or organizational policy deployment.
documents.audience: AI governance, platform, and risk teams
documents.body:
# Authority Model

Object status: **Methodology**.

## Chain

1) **HUMAN (authority source)**
2) **FREEZE RULES (default constraint)**
3) **AGENTS (bounded executors)**

## Override Contract

Overrides are permitted only if:
- The target is identified.
- The justification is explicit.
- The override is logged.
- The resulting state is re-validated by gates.

## What This Controls

- Silent policy drift.
- Undocumented escalation.
- Model behavior that rewrites intent without review.

documents.title: VERSION AND RELEASE LIFECYCLE
documents.description: Research artefact describing how governed changes become auditable versions rather than silent overwrites.
documents.claim_supported: Governed AI outputs need version lifecycle semantics so changes remain reviewable and release-aware.
documents.boundary: Not a live changelog service, release management system, or enterprise audit ledger.
documents.body:
# Version Lifecycle

Object status: **Research artefact**.

## Two Modes

### Incremental
v4.0 → v4.1 → v4.1.1 → v4.1.2 → v4.2 → v4.3
Patches and exceptions accumulate. Edge cases are documented.

### Consolidated
v5.0 absorbs prior deltas into a self-explaining baseline:
- Reduced exception load
- Unified semantics
- Fewer conditional branches

## Rule
Consolidation is not cosmetic. Consolidation is a governance event that changes release eligibility.

documents.title: FREEZE AND ESCALATION SEMANTICS
documents.description: Conceptual control model for when a pipeline must stop, repair, escalate, or preserve an audit trail.
documents.claim_supported: Model-behavior risk can be made legible through explicit freeze, repair, escalation, and audit states.
documents.boundary: Not evidence of automated enforcement in production infrastructure.
documents.audience: Risk reviewers, AI operators, and interface designers
documents.body:
# Freeze Semantics

Object status: **Conceptual architecture**.

## State Progression

- **FROZEN**: release is suspended.
- **REPAIR**: bounded revision is permitted.
- **ESCALATE**: human review is required.
- **AUDIT**: the state transition and artifact trail are preserved.

## UI Mapping

Frozen state is visible:
- Particle flow halts.
- Track dims to 10% opacity.
- A single static dot remains centered.

The system does not disappear. It enters reviewable stillness.

documents.title: GOVERNED PROTOTYPE FLOW
documents.description: Prototype evidence for how intent, model work, validation, and artifact release can be represented through a gated pipeline.
documents.claim_supported: A governed agent flow can be represented as inspectable movement through actors, checkpoints, and audit states.
documents.boundary: Not production orchestration, live model execution, or customer deployment proof.
documents.body:
# Pipeline Overview

Object status: **Prototype evidence**.

## Pre-Pipeline
Modeled inputs:
- session_state
- context
- authority boundary

Pre-step concepts:
- skeleton population
- session state normalization
- release condition registration

## Main Pipeline (actors)
S1 Communicator -> S2 Mediator -> S3 Drafter -> S4 Refiner -> S5 Origin

## Modeled Outputs
- updated_session_state
- delta_brief (human-readable)
- artifact reference(s)
- audit state

## Principle
A joint represents work. A gate represents validation. They are never the same object.

documents.title: JOINT HANDOFF SPECIFICATIONS
documents.description: Methodology for constraining agent handoffs so unvalidated work does not move downstream.
documents.claim_supported: Agent handoffs should be explicit contracts between stages rather than implicit conversational continuation.
documents.boundary: Not proof of an implemented workflow engine or automated integration layer.
documents.audience: Agent system designers and platform teams
documents.body:
# Joint Specifications

Object status: **Methodology**.

## S1 — Communicator
Intent → Plan
Produces: execution_plan, job_ticket

## S2 — Mediator
Plan → Constraint Packet
Produces: constraints_packet

## S3 — Drafter
Constraints → Draft Artifact
Produces: drafter_output

## S4 — Refiner
Draft → Patchlist + Verdict
Produces: patchlist (verdict: pass|fail)

## S5 — Origin
Commit on pass
Produces: origin_commit, updated session_state, delta_brief

## Retry Policy
On fail: route back to Drafter. Retry ≤ 2. Escalate after bound reached.

documents.title: GOVERNANCE GATE SPECIFICATIONS
documents.description: Conceptual checkpoints for representing how risky model behavior should be stopped before downstream propagation.
documents.claim_supported: Validation gates create explicit decision points for pass, fail, repair, escalation, and release eligibility.
documents.boundary: Not a certified control framework, compliance attestation, or production policy engine.
documents.body:
# Gate Specifications

Object status: **Conceptual architecture**.

| Gate  | Location         | Validates              | On Fail                  |
|-------|------------------|------------------------|---------------------------|
| G_IDEM | Pipeline entry  | composite_hash match   | return cached output      |
| G1    | post-Communicator| execution_plan schema  | retry (max 2)             |
| G1A   | post-Adapter     | XML validity           | escalate                  |
| G2    | post-Mediator    | constraints_packet     | retry (max 2)             |
| G4    | post-Refiner     | verdict validity       | route by verdict          |

## UI Rule
Gate diamonds activate before their downstream joint. The checkpoint is the last visible boundary before the prototype represents progression.

documents.title: HUMAN CONTROL SURFACE
documents.description: Planned interface direction for keeping authority, state, escalation, and release eligibility visible to a human reviewer.
documents.claim_supported: The governance model should become a human-readable control surface before it becomes a product surface.
documents.boundary: Not an implemented authenticated surface, live backend, or operational control room.
documents.audience: Product strategists, governance leads, and interface reviewers
documents.body:
# Orchestration Manual

Object status: **Planned interface direction**.

## Human Control Surface
The human authors session_state and selects routing in the governance model. Agents do not self-author authority.

## Modeled Procedure (high level)
1) Human provides session_state + context
2) System is represented as entering at G_IDEM
3) Joints and gates are represented with schema constraints
4) Origin commit semantics are modeled on pass
5) Human receives delta_brief concept

## Non-negotiable
Session state is the contract boundary. Any future mutation would need to be logged and validated.

documents.title: DIRECT UI MEMBRANE VISUAL SPEC
documents.description: Documentation-only visual specification for the planned Direct UI Membrane concept.
documents.claim_supported: Facade Reflection 0.4 records the accepted visual direction for a future controlled human-review membrane.
documents.boundary: Documentation-only and non-operational; not an implemented authenticated surface, no live AI execution, no customer data, no production deployment, no operational release authority, and no compliance certification.
documents.related_pipeline_stage: Planned Direct UI Membrane review concept
documents.audience: Product strategists, governance leads, interface reviewers, and documentation reviewers
documents.body:
# Direct UI Membrane Visual Spec

Object status: **Concept-stage visual specification**.

## Reference
`docs/FACADE_REFLECTION_0_4_DIRECT_UI_MEMBRANE_VISUAL_SPEC.md`

## Scope
Facade Reflection 0.4 documents the accepted visual direction for the planned Direct UI Membrane. It is documentation-only and non-operational.

AETHERUS_MONOLITH / Section 1.1 remains the instantiated static public artifact. Section 1.2 Direct UI Membrane remains planned and specified only.

## Boundary
This card does not implement Section 1.2. It does not add runtime UI behavior, routes, authentication, backend routes, database behavior, persistence, billing, public NEXUS execution, Palisade, or Weave.

## Explicit Non-Claims
- not an implemented authenticated surface
- no live AI execution
- no customer data
- no production deployment
- no operational release authority
- no compliance certification

documents.title: AUDIT ARTIFACT CATALOG
documents.description: Research artefact mapping producers, consumers, and formats for traceable governed-output records.
documents.claim_supported: Auditability depends on named artifacts with producers, consumers, and formats.
documents.boundary: Not a live evidence repository, storage layer, or immutable audit ledger.
documents.audience: Documentation, governance, and platform architecture reviewers
documents.body:
# Artifact Catalog

Object status: **Research artefact**.

| Artifact          | Producer     | Consumer    | Format         |
|-------------------|--------------|-------------|----------------|
| session_state     | Origin       | all joints  | XML            |
| execution_plan    | Communicator | Mediator    | XML            |
| job_ticket        | Communicator | Mediator+   | XML (canonical)|
| constraints_packet| Mediator     | Drafter     | XML            |
| drafter_output    | Drafter      | Refiner     | XML            |
| patchlist         | Refiner      | Origin      | XML            |
| origin_commit     | Origin       | Orchestrator| XML            |
| delta_brief       | Origin       | Human       | human-readable |

## Human Exception
delta_brief is the only artifact addressed to a human.

documents.title: RISK PROTOCOL CATALOG
documents.description: Index of prototype protocols for extracting constraints, building state, and preserving reviewable output.
documents.claim_supported: Governed AI work needs repeatable protocols for state generation, constraint extraction, and output review.
documents.boundary: Not a finalized standards library or external compliance framework.
documents.audience: Research reviewers and AI workflow designers
documents.body:
# Protocol Catalog

Object status: **Research artefact**.

- Skeleton Population Protocol — v1.0
- Session State Generator — v1.0
- Universal Project Brief — v1.4
- Prompt Templates — v2.1

Protocol documents are self-contained: PRIME DIRECTIVE → SOURCE HIERARCHY → EXTRACTION RULES → PROCEDURE → OUTPUT SCHEMA → SPECIAL CASES → ANTI-PATTERNS.

documents.title: CONTROL TEMPLATE INDEX
documents.description: Methodology index for templates, joints, triggers, and deterministic routing entry points.
documents.claim_supported: Templates can constrain model work by defining permitted structure, trigger conditions, and stage ownership.
documents.boundary: Not a complete prompt library, runtime template engine, or validated automation suite.
documents.audience: Prompt system designers and governance reviewers
documents.body:
# Template Index

Object status: **Methodology**.

| Template                        | Joint        | Trigger             |
|---------------------------------|--------------|---------------------|
| T_comm_execution_plan           | Communicator | user intent         |
| T_comm_error_escalation         | Communicator | escalate verdict    |
| T_mediator_constraints_packet   | Mediator     | job_ticket          |
| T_drafter_constrained_output    | Drafter      | constraints_packet  |
| T_refiner_patchlist             | Refiner      | drafter_output      |
| T_origin_commit                 | Origin       | verdict:pass        |

Templates are contracts. They encode permitted structure and disallow improvisation.

documents.title: SCHEMA TRACEABILITY MODEL
documents.description: Conceptual schema inventory for making model-produced artifacts reviewable before release.
documents.claim_supported: Schema expectations make model-produced artifacts easier to validate before release.
documents.boundary: Not a complete XSD package, production validator, or certified data contract.
documents.body:
# Schema Definitions

Object status: **Conceptual architecture**.

## Inventory

- session_state.xsd
- execution_plan.xsd
- constraints_packet.xsd
- drafter_output.xsd
- patchlist.xsd
- origin_commit.xsd

## Principle
In the model, if it cannot be validated, it should not proceed.

documents.title: DELTA BRIEF RECORD
documents.description: Illustrative human-readable audit record: what changed, why, what remains frozen, and what must be reviewed.
documents.claim_supported: Human-readable deltas can summarize what changed, why, what remains frozen, and what requires review.
documents.boundary: Not a live audit log, compliance record, or customer deployment report.
documents.body:
# delta_brief (Human Output)

Object status: **Prototype evidence**.

## Purpose
Summarize a modeled delta without ambiguity.

## Required fields
- What changed (scope)
- Why it changed (justification)
- What remains frozen (boundaries)
- Verification required (if any)

## Tone
No celebration. No apology. Clear finality.
